message.content:
T1 — R3-01: Per-category PBR (materials.ts only)

  ▎ Start with: git checkout -b r3-01-pbr-tuning

  You are working on packages/web/src/viewport/materials.ts in a BIM (architectural CAD)
  web app. There are no other files to touch.

  CONTEXT
  =======
  The file currently exports resolveCategoryMaterial(), which returns a
  CategoryMaterialSpec for each element category (wall, floor, roof, door,
  window, stair, railing, room, site, section, sheet).

  Right now ALL categories share a single DEFAULT_PBR object:
    const DEFAULT_PBR = { roughness: 0.85, metalness: 0, aoIntensity: 0.4 }

  This means wall, railing, glazing etc. all get roughness=0.85, metalness=0.
  That is wrong. Railing should be metallic steel; glazing near-mirror; etc.

  There is also a ResolveOptions interface with optional roughness/metalness/
  aoIntensity overrides used by tests — keep that working.

  TASK
  ====
  1. Add a PER_CATEGORY_PBR lookup table constant (typed as
     Record<ElementCategoryToken, { roughness: number; metalness: number }>)
     with these values (from the rendering spec):

     wall:     roughness 0.80, metalness 0.00
     floor:    roughness 0.90, metalness 0.00
     roof:     roughness 0.85, metalness 0.00
     door:     roughness 0.70, metalness 0.00
     window:   roughness 0.60, metalness 0.05   ← window FRAME (not glazing)
     stair:    roughness 0.85, metalness 0.00
     railing:  roughness 0.35, metalness 0.65   ← brushed stainless
     room:     roughness 0.85, metalness 0.00   ← no 3D mesh but needs a value
     site:     roughness 0.95, metalness 0.00
     section:  roughness 0.85, metalness 0.00
     sheet:    roughness 0.85, metalness 0.00

  2. Add CSS token reading for per-category PBR overrides. Before falling back
     to the table above, try to read --cat-CATEGORY-roughness and
     --cat-CATEGORY-metalness via the TokenReader. Use parseFloat(); if the
     result is NaN fall through to the table value. Example:

       const rawR = reader.read(`--cat-${category}-roughness`);
       const roughness = rawR !== null && !Number.isNaN(parseFloat(rawR))
         ? parseFloat(rawR)
         : PER_CATEGORY_PBR[category].roughness;

  3. Update resolveCategoryMaterial() to use the per-category roughness/metalness
     instead of DEFAULT_PBR. Keep the ResolveOptions.roughness / .metalness
     OVERRIDES working — they take highest priority (used by tests that pin
     specific values).

     Priority order (highest first):
       options.roughness  → if provided, use it
       CSS token          → if token present and parseable, use it
       PER_CATEGORY_PBR   → always defined

  4. aoIntensity stays at DEFAULT_PBR.aoIntensity (0.4) for all categories
     unless overridden via ResolveOptions.aoIntensity.

  5. Update packages/web/src/viewport/materials.test.ts:
     - The test "reads --cat-* tokens for every category" currently asserts
       expect(spec.roughness).toBe(0.85) and expect(spec.metalness).toBe(0)
       for ALL categories. Replace these with per-category assert

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
All 10 `materials.test.ts` tests pass. The single failure is a pre-existing unrelated test in `Viewport3DLayersPanel.test.tsx` (an i18next missing-provider issue, not touched by this task).

**Summary of changes:**

**`materials.ts`:**
- Added `PER_CATEGORY_PBR` table with per-category `roughness`/`metalness` values from the rendering spec (railing at 0.35/0.65, window frame at 0.60/0.05, site at 0.95/0.00, etc.)
- `resolveCategoryMaterial()` now reads `--cat-{category}-roughness` and `--cat-{category}-metalness` CSS tokens via the `TokenReader`, with priority: `options.*` → CSS token → `PER_CATEGORY_PBR`
- `aoIntensity` remains fixed at `DEFAULT_PBR.aoIntensity` (0.4) unless overridden via `ResolveOptions`

**`materials.test.ts`:**
- Replaced the flat `expect(spec.roughness).toBe(0.85)` / `expect(spec.metalness).toBe(0)` assertions with a `PBR_EXPECTED` lookup table that asserts the correct per-category values for each of the 11 categories
